welcomeMessage.en: Today, we are focusing deeply on our daily routines and how we manage our precious time.
welcomeMessage.zh: 今天，我们将深入探讨我们的日常生活习惯以及如何管理宝贵的时间。

welcomeMessage.en: Time management is one of the most common topics in both casual conversations and professional settings.
welcomeMessage.zh: 时间管理是日常交流和职场中最常见的话题之一。

welcomeMessage.en: Whether you are a night owl or an early bird, exploring how we structure our day is a great way to practice active verbs and sequencing adverbs (like 'first', 'then', and 'finally').
welcomeMessage.zh: 无论你是夜猫子还是早起的人，探讨我们如何安排一天是练习动词和顺序副词的好方法。

welcomeMessage.en: Take a moment before class to mentally walk through your typical day. Let's reflect on the habits that make us feel most productive!
welcomeMessage.zh: 课前花点时间在脑海中回顾一下你典型的一天。让我们反思一下哪些习惯能让我们感到最高效！

questionsToThinkAbout.en: What does a typical morning look like for you, step by step?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 一步步来说，你典型的早晨日常是怎样的？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: Are you more productive in the morning or late at night, and why do you think that is?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 你在早上还是深夜的工作效率更高？你觉得为什么？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: How do you organize your schedule to make sure you get everything done? Do you use apps, calendars, or a paper to-do list?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 你如何安排你的日程表以确保完成所有任务？你会使用应用程序、日历还是纸质待办事项清单？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: What is your biggest distraction when you are trying to work or study?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 当你在工作或学习时，最大的干扰是什么？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: If you could add one healthy habit to your daily routine, what would it be?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 如果你能在日常生活中增加一个健康的习惯，那会是什么？

vocabulary.definition: A sequence of actions regularly followed.
vocabulary.example: "My morning routine always includes stretching and drinking coffee."

vocabulary.definition: The state or quality of producing something effectively.
vocabulary.example: "Turning off my phone greatly improves my productivity."

vocabulary.definition: A person who rises, arrives, or acts before the usual expected time.
vocabulary.example: "My mom is an early bird; she wakes up at 5 AM every day!"

vocabulary.definition: A person who is active late at night.
vocabulary.example: "As a night owl, I find it easiest to focus on my writing after midnight."

vocabulary.definition: To arrange into a structured whole; order.
vocabulary.example: "I like to organize my desk before starting work."

vocabulary.definition: A settled or regular tendency or practice.
vocabulary.example: "Reading before bed is a very healthy habit."

vocabulary.definition: To delay or postpone action; put off doing something.
vocabulary.example: "I tend to procrastinate when I am faced with a difficult task."

vocabulary.definition: The ability to use one's time effectively or productively.
vocabulary.example: "Good time management is essential for balancing work and family."

vocabulary.definition: To determine the order for dealing with a series of items according to their relative importance.
vocabulary.translation: 优先考虑 / 按轻重缓急安排
vocabulary.example: "When I am busy, I have to prioritize my most important tasks first."

vocabulary.definition: To have too much to deal with.
vocabulary.translation: 不知所措 / 不堪重负
vocabulary.example: "I feel overwhelmed when I have three exams in one week."

vocabulary.definition: To deal with more than one task at the same time.
vocabulary.example: "Some people are good at multitasking, but I prefer to focus on one thing."

meaning.en: To stay awake late at night to work or study.
meaning.zh: 为了工作或学习而熬夜。

commonPhrases.example: "I have a big presentation tomorrow, so I need to burn the midnight oil tonight."

meaning.en: To finish something before the time is up.
meaning.zh: 在时间用完之前完成某事。

commonPhrases.example: "We managed to beat the clock and submit the project just in time!"

meaning.en: Very active or busy; moving about.
meaning.zh: 非常活跃或忙碌；在路上。

commonPhrases.example: "I don't have time for a proper breakfast, so I just eat on the go."

goals: Encourage flow: Focus heavily on transition words (then, after that, finally) when they describe their routine.
Grammar focus (mental note): Watch out for the correct usage of Present Simple for habits and routines.
Expand vocabulary: If they say 'I wake up, I eat, I work', encourage them to use stronger verbs and adverbs!

phases.title: Phase 1: Warm-Up Questions (10-15 Minutes)
questions: How is your day going so far?
What is the very first thing you do when you wake up in the morning?
Do you ever hit the 'snooze' button on your alarm?

phases.title: Phase 2: Deep Dive Questions (30-40 Minutes)
phases.description: Encourage detailed storytelling about their lifestyle.
questions: Walk me through your perfect morning routine on a weekend.
Do you consider yourself a night owl or an early bird? Why do you think that is?
What is your biggest distraction when you are trying to be productive?
How do you organize your tasks? Do you use a written planner, an app, or just keep it in your head?
If you could add one extra healthy habit to your daily routine, what would it be?

phases.title: Phase 3: Fun Roleplay (5-10 Minutes)
phases.description: If you have extra time or want to make them laugh, try this scenario.
phases.scenario: Tell your friend: 'Imagine I am your new life coach. I am deeply unorganized and chaotic. I drink 10 coffees a day and sleep for 2 hours. Your job is to give me a strict, healthy routine to fix my life!'
